TRACK_CHUNK: Track each chunk with the plan (`update_plan`).
DECOMPOSE_LINE: Break the work into small, independent pieces. Track them with the plan.
APPROACH_STEP4: **Match the Right Skill**: Pull in the `/skill:<name>` that fits each chunk
COLLAB_SECTION: - Specialist personas are installed as Agent Skills, invoked as `/skill:<name>` (e.g. `/skill:python-backend`, `/skill:code-reviewer`, `/skill:commit`), and can also load automatically from their description.

- Reach for the skill that matches the work: `/skill:python-backend` for Python services, `/skill:frontend-engineer-ts` for React/TypeScript UI, `/skill:cdk-expert-python` or `/skill:cdk-expert-ts` for infrastructure, `/skill:python-test-engineer` or `/skill:typescript-test-engineer` for tests, `/skill:code-reviewer` before finishing, etc.
- Give each invocation a clear, self-contained instruction with the context it needs — file paths, requirements, constraints, and expected output.

- When work splits into independent pieces, sequence them through the plan (use the `pi-subagents` package to delegate to parallel/chained subagents). Do not over-decompose tightly-coupled work — sequential execution is fine.
EXTRA_SECTION: - This install ships specialist personas as Agent Skills. Invoke one with
task matches its specialism, and follow the loaded SKILL.md instructions.
- Pi has no MCP. When you need an external capability, prefer a well-documented
CLI tool (with a README the agent can read) or a Skill over reaching for a
- A `pi-checks` extension runs tests, lint, type-checks and security scans after
file edits and at the end of a turn. Its output is advisory — read the
findings and fix real issues, but it will not block you.
